summary: Chromium cookie/password infostealer with obfuscated remote-gated payload
details: Package ships a Windows-only native addon (build/Release/debugelevator.node) together with a bytenode-compiled, js-confuser-obfuscated JS bundle at src/license.jsc. The native addon attaches as a debugger to chrome.exe / msedge.exe / brave.exe, kills any running instances, sets hardware execution breakpoints inside chrome.dll / msedge.dll, and reads the App-Bound AES key directly out of browser process memory via ReadProcessMemory; the JS layer then decrypts the local Chromium Cookies and Login Data stores (cookies and saved passwords). The obfuscated bundle fetches a raw GitHub URL at runtime to retrieve a key allowlist, caches it under os.tmpdir(), and conditionally loads the native addon — giving the author a post-publish kill-switch and gating mechanism. The exports actually present in the bytenode bundle (generateApiKey, validateLicenseKey, requireLicense, getValidKeys) do not match the README's documented cookie/password API, and package.json declares main='index.js' which require()s a non-existent './index.jsc' — a cover-story 'license' framing layered over offensive infostealer functionality. Repository/bugs/homepage point at the placeholder github.com/yourusername/baksen, so there is no authoritative source for the obfuscated bundle. Installing or loading this package gives the author an infostealer running against the installer's local Chromium browsers.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
